channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Cancels Negotiator Trip
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned trip by envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks, citing inadequate Iranian proposals. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters a stalemate phase.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Campaign Targeting Politicians
item.description: The German government believes Russia orchestrated phishing attacks via the Signal messaging app targeting high-profile politicians, diplomats, military officers, and journalists. At least 300 accounts were compromised in the cyber campaign.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Apartment Building in Yekaterinburg
item.description: A Ukrainian drone hit a multi-story apartment building in Russia's fourth-largest city of Yekaterinburg, marking the first attack on the city since the conflict began. Regional authorities reported damage to residential infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Colombia Bus Bombing Kills 13 Ahead of Presidential Election
item.description: At least 13 people were killed and 17 wounded in an explosives attack on a bus in western Colombia. Authorities blamed dissidents of the FARC guerrilla group for the violence amid escalating pre-election tensions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott Over Russian Athlete Decision
item.description: Finland announced it will not host international championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in protest against the governing body's policy.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Hezbollah Sabotage Disrupts Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Talks
item.description: Hezbollah's continued attacks threaten to derail US-mediated ceasefire negotiations between Israel and Lebanon. The militant group's actions come despite an extended truce agreement.
item.dc:creator: The Defense Post

item.title: Ukraine Deploys Anubis and Seth Drones Against Russian Forces
item.description: Ukraine's military has begun fielding new Anubis and Seth X-series drones in its fight against Russian forces. The advanced UAVs represent a significant upgrade in Ukraine's drone warfare capabilities.
item.dc:creator: The Defense Post

item.title: Chinese Experts: Iran Detected and Damaged US F-35 Stealth Jet
item.description: Chinese military analysts claim Iran successfully detected and damaged a US F-35 stealth fighter during recent combat operations, raising questions about the aircraft's survivability in contested airspace.
item.dc:creator: South China Morning Post

item.title: Russia Could Be Ready for NATO Conflict Year After Ukraine, Dutch Warn
item.description: Dutch intelligence warns that Russia could be prepared for a conflict with NATO within a year after concluding its war in Ukraine. The assessment highlights growing concerns about Russian military reconstitution.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: Thailand and Cambodia Sign Ceasefire Under Trump Pressure
item.description: Thailand and Cambodia signed a ceasefire agreement amid heightened US pressure from the Trump administration. The deal aims to de-escalate tensions between the Southeast Asian neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Daily Sabah

item.title: Russian Defence Minister Visits North Korea
item.description: Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for discussions, the second high-level visit in recent months. The trip highlights deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Trump at White House Correspondents Dinner After Shooting Incident
item.description: President Trump attended the White House Correspondents' Association dinner following a security incident where an armed suspect attempted to breach the venue. A Secret Service agent was wounded in the shooting.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: Macron Reaffirms Efforts to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron reiterated efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months. The strategic waterway remains blocked amid ongoing hostilities.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Egypt Economic Outlook Trimmed Due to Iran War
item.description: Analysts have lowered forecasts for Egyptian economic growth as the Iran war drives up energy prices and puts pressure on inflation. The regional spillover effects continue to impact neighboring economies.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Greece-France Defense Deal Strengthens NATO Mediterranean Presence
item.description: A new defense agreement between Greece and France enhances NATO's Mediterranean presence as European nations bolster military cooperation amid growing regional threats.
item.dc:creator: Washington Post

item.title: White House Correspondents Dinner Security Raises Questions After Shooting
item.description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner has raised renewed questions about security for presidential events. An armed suspect was taken into custody after wounding a Secret Service agent.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Pope Francis Appeals for Peace in Global Conflicts
item.description: Pope Francis renewed his appeal for peace amid ongoing global conflicts, calling for dialogue and humanitarian assistance in war-torn regions around the world.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Putin Faces Growing War Discontent in Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin faces mounting domestic discontent over the Ukraine war as casualties mount and economic pressures increase. Approval ratings show signs of strain after years of conflict.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Global Leaders Address Iran Conflict at UN
item.description: World leaders gathered at the United Nations to address the ongoing Iran conflict, discussing diplomatic solutions and humanitarian concerns as the war continues.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Middle East Tensions Escalate Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Tensions continue to escalate across the Middle East as the Iran conflict enters a new phase, with regional powers recalibrating their positions and alliances.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Colombia Highway Bomb Attack Kills Several Ahead of Election
item.description: A bomb attack on a highway in Colombia killed several people ahead of the presidential election, raising concerns about election security and ongoing violence in the country.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Japan Shocks China in Taiwan Strait Incident
item.description: Japan's unexpected military action in the Taiwan Strait has drawn sharp reactions from China, escalating tensions in one of the world's most contested waterways.
item.dc:creator: Chiang Rai Times

item.title: Ukraine War Developments: Frontline Updates
item.description: Latest updates from the Ukraine-Russia front lines as Ukrainian forces continue defensive operations against ongoing Russian military pressure.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Post

item.title: Ukraine Military Situation Report
item.description: Detailed analysis of the current military situation in Ukraine as Russian forces intensify operations along multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Post

item.title: Spain Affirms NATO Commitment Amid US Pressure Reports
item.description: Spain's Prime Minister affirmed the country's reliability as a NATO member following reports of US pressure regarding defense spending commitments.
item.dc:creator: Strait Times
